zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Tabley於1296年出生在英國爾塔波時，她的父親Adam d'Eiville - Lord of Tabley and Cheadle46歲，母親Beatrix de Bexton22歲。她於大約1312年與Thomas d'Anvers II在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1330年在她的家鄉享年34歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tabley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Over and Nether Tabley (Cheshire).

variants.name: Tapley
meanings: habitational name from Tapeley in Devon. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Tæppa + Old English lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’.
variant of Tabley, a habitational name from Over and Nether Tabley in Cheshire. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Tæbba + Old English lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Tabley was born in 1296, in Tabley, Cheshire, England, her father, Adam d'Eiville - Lord of Tabley and Cheadle, was 46 and her mother, Beatrix de Bexton, was 22. She married Thomas d'Anvers II about 1312, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1330, in her hometown, at the age of 34.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Tabley(이)가 1296년 영국 체실르 타블리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam d'Eiville - Lord of Tabley and Cheadle(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrix de Bexton(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Thomas d'Anvers II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1330년 그녀의 고향에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas d'Anvers II aproximadamente 1312, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1330, em sua cidade natal, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Tabley родилась в 1296 г. в местности табли, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Adam d'Eiville - Lord of Tabley and Cheadle было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Bexton, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas d'Anvers II около 1312 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1330 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 34 лет.
